title: Rhetoric and persuasion
canDo: I can argue persuasively with rhetorical control.

chunks.text: It would be remiss not to mention the cost.
chunks.use: Raise a point you must not omit.
chunks.example: It would be remiss not to mention the cost.

chunks.text: One cannot overstate its importance.
chunks.example: One cannot overstate its importance.

chunks.text: Far be it from me to criticise.
chunks.example: Far be it from me to criticise, but...

chunks.text: She made a compelling case.
chunks.example: She made a compelling case for reform.

chunks.text: The crux of the matter is trust.
chunks.example: The crux of the matter is trust.

grammar.pattern: It would be remiss not to... / One cannot overstate...
examples: It would be remiss not to thank them.

reading.passage: In her address, the director made a compelling case for reform. One cannot overstate its importance, she argued. The crux of the matter is trust. It would be remiss not to mention the cost, though far be it from her to dismiss the concerns of staff.

questions.q: What did the director argue for?
What is the crux of the matter?

script.t: Yes, she made a compelling case.

dictogloss.sentence: The crux of the matter is trust.

fluency432.prompt: Make a persuasive case for a change you believe in.

task.prompt: Argue persuasively for a reform, acknowledging a concern.

writing.model: She made a compelling case for reform. One cannot overstate its importance. The crux of the matter is trust, though it would be remiss not to address the cost.

sentenceCombining.answer: She made a compelling case, and trust is central to it.
It would be remiss not to mention the cost.

task.prompt: Write a persuasive paragraph (4 sentences) arguing for a change.

quiz.q: Stress that something is hugely important.

options: The crux of the matter is trust.
It would be remiss not to.

title: Connotation and tone
canDo: I can detect and convey tone and connotation.

chunks.text: There's an undertone of doubt.
chunks.example: There's an undertone of doubt.

chunks.text: It carries negative connotations.
chunks.example: It carries negative connotations.

chunks.text: To put it mildly, it was tense.
chunks.example: To put it mildly, it was tense.

chunks.text: That's loaded language.
chunks.example: That's loaded language.

chunks.text: She said it with a hint of irony.
chunks.example: She said it with a hint of irony.

grammar.pattern: It carries... connotations / with a hint of irony
examples: He smiled with a hint of irony.

reading.passage: The review praised the film, but there was an undertone of doubt. Words like adequate carry negative connotations. To put it mildly, the ending divided viewers. The critic called it ambitious, with a hint of irony, and warned readers about loaded language in the marketing.

questions.q: What did the review's tone hint at?
How did viewers feel about the ending?

vocabInContext.q: 'to put it mildly' suggests the reality was…

script.t: There was an undertone of doubt.

gap.q: She said it with a hint of ___ .

fluency432.prompt: Describe a review and the tone behind its words.

retell.prompt: Summarise the review and its tone.

task.prompt: Describe how a word's tone changes its meaning.

writing.model: There was an undertone of doubt. Words like adequate carry negative connotations. To put it mildly, the ending divided viewers, and the praise came with a hint of irony.

sentenceCombining.answer: The word seems neutral, yet it carries negative connotations.
To put it mildly, it was tense.

task.prompt: Analyse the tone of a piece of writing (4 sentences).

quiz.q: Hint that reality was worse than stated.
options: To put it mildly, it was tense.

title: Rigorous argument
canDo: I can analyse and critique an argument's logic.

chunks.text: It's predicated on one assumption.
chunks.example: It's predicated on one assumption.

chunks.text: The whole plan hinges on funding.
chunks.example: The whole plan hinges on funding.

chunks.text: That's a false dichotomy.
chunks.example: That's a false dichotomy.

chunks.text: Let me play devil's advocate.
chunks.example: Let me play devil's advocate.

chunks.text: By the same token, it could fail.
chunks.use: Apply the same logic in reverse.
chunks.example: By the same token, it could fail.

grammar.pattern: It hinges on... / By the same token... / a false dichotomy
examples: By the same token, the reverse is true.

reading.passage: The proposal is predicated on continued growth. The whole plan hinges on funding. Critics frame it as a choice between speed and safety, but that is a false dichotomy. Let me play devil's advocate: by the same token, doing nothing also carries real risk.

questions.q: What does the plan depend on?
What does the writer call the speed-or-safety framing?

dictogloss.sentence: The whole plan hinges on funding.

fluency432.prompt: Argue a position, then play devil's advocate against it.

retell.prompt: Summarise the critique of the proposal.

task.prompt: Critique an argument: expose an assumption or a false dichotomy.

writing.model: The proposal is predicated on growth and hinges on funding. The speed-versus-safety framing is a false dichotomy. To play devil's advocate, by the same token, inaction is also risky.

sentenceCombining.answer: The whole plan hinges on funding.
It seems a clear choice, but it is a false dichotomy.

task.prompt: Critically analyse an argument (4 sentences), naming an assumption.

quiz.q: Say a plan depends entirely on one thing.
options: The whole plan hinges on funding.

title: Concession and nuance
canDo: I can concede and qualify with sophistication.

chunks.text: Granted, it has flaws.
chunks.example: Granted, it has flaws.

chunks.text: That is not to say it failed.
chunks.example: That is not to say it failed.

chunks.text: It worked, albeit slowly.
chunks.example: It worked, albeit slowly.

chunks.text: Notwithstanding the cost, it helps.
chunks.example: Notwithstanding the cost, it helps.

chunks.text: For all its flaws, it's useful.
chunks.example: For all its flaws, it's useful.

examples: It works, albeit at a price.

reading.passage: Granted, the software has flaws. That is not to say it failed; it solved the core problem, albeit slowly. Notwithstanding the cost, it saved hundreds of hours. For all its flaws, it remains the best tool we have tried.

questions.q: Does the writer think the software failed?
What is the writer's overall verdict?

script.t: That is not to say it failed.

fluency432.prompt: Evaluate something fairly: concede flaws, then defend it.

retell.prompt: Summarise the balanced verdict on the software.

task.prompt: Give a balanced verdict, conceding weaknesses but defending overall.

writing.model: Granted, the tool has flaws. That is not to say it failed; it worked, albeit slowly. Notwithstanding the cost, for all its flaws, it remains the best option.

sentenceCombining.answer: For all its flaws, it is still useful.

task.prompt: Write a balanced evaluation (4 sentences) with concession.

options: For all its flaws, it's useful.
That is not to say it failed.

title: Idiom and subtlety
canDo: I can use sophisticated idioms naturally.

chunks.text: You have to read between the lines.
chunks.example: You have to read between the lines.

chunks.text: It was a blessing in disguise.
chunks.use: A bad event with a good result.
chunks.example: It was a blessing in disguise.

chunks.text: Let's address the elephant in the room.
chunks.example: Let's address the elephant in the room.

chunks.text: It's a double-edged sword.
chunks.example: It's a double-edged sword.

chunks.text: Take it with a pinch of salt.
chunks.example: Take it with a pinch of salt.

reading.passage: Losing that contract felt terrible, but it was a blessing in disguise. To understand the report, you have to read between the lines. Automation is a double-edged sword. Finally, let's address the elephant in the room: morale. And take the rosy forecasts with a pinch of salt.

questions.q: How does the writer view losing the contract now?
What is the elephant in the room?

script.t: Actually, it was a blessing in disguise.

fluency432.prompt: Describe a setback that turned out well, using idioms.

retell.prompt: Summarise the briefing using the idioms.

task.prompt: Discuss a tricky situation using two idioms naturally.

writing.model: Losing it was a blessing in disguise. To grasp the report, read between the lines. Automation is a double-edged sword, so take the forecasts with a pinch of salt.

sentenceCombining.answer: Losing the contract was a blessing in disguise.
It is a double-edged sword, with both benefits and drawbacks.

task.prompt: Write about a setback that became positive (4 sentences), using an idiom.

quiz.q: Say a bad event turned out well.
options: It was a blessing in disguise.
Take it with a pinch of salt.

title: Register and precision
canDo: I can express precise positions in a high register.

chunks.text: I would contend that it's flawed.
chunks.example: I would contend that it's flawed.

chunks.text: Ostensibly, it's about safety.
chunks.example: Ostensibly, it's about safety.

chunks.text: By and large, it succeeds.
chunks.example: By and large, it succeeds.

chunks.text: She said so in no uncertain terms.
chunks.example: She said so in no uncertain terms.

chunks.text: To all intents and purposes, it's done.
chunks.example: To all intents and purposes, it's done.

grammar.pattern: I would contend that... / ostensibly / by and large
examples: Ostensibly neutral, it favours one side.

reading.passage: I would contend that the rule is flawed. Ostensibly, it is about safety, but by and large it protects profit. The committee rejected it in no uncertain terms. To all intents and purposes, the old policy is finished, even if it remains on paper.

questions.q: What is the writer's view of the rule?

script.t: Ostensibly, yes, but by and large it protects profit.

gist.q: Does B think it's really about safety?

fluency432.prompt: Argue a formal position, distinguishing appearance from reality.

retell.prompt: Summarise the formal critique of the rule.

task.prompt: Make a precise formal claim, distinguishing the stated aim from the real one.

writing.model: I would contend that the rule is flawed. Ostensibly about safety, by and large it protects profit. The committee rejected it in no uncertain terms; to all intents and purposes, it is finished.

parts: It claims to be about safety.
sentenceCombining.answer: Ostensibly about safety, it really protects profit.

task.prompt: Write a precise formal argument (4 sentences) on a policy.

quiz.q: Make a formal claim or argument.
options: I would contend that it's flawed.

quiz.q: Note the surface versus the real purpose.
